content.text:
<analysis>
a. Event Type: General comment on a PR requesting code review
b. Request Type: Code review - the user is asking for review of a new disorder curation (Larsen Syndrome) focusing on curation quality, evidence integrity, ontology precision, and schema alignment
c. Key Information:
   - PR adds manual curation for Larsen Syndrome (MONDO:0007875)
   - Includes 12 new files: main disorder YAML, 9 PubMed cache files, 2 research documentation files
   - Author claims validation passes for schema, references, and terms
   - Claims 100% weighted compliance score
   - Manual deep research was done due to automated research tool issues

d. Main Tasks:
   - Review the Larsen_Syndrome.yaml file for curation quality
   - Validate evidence integrity (PMID references, snippets)
   - Check ontology term precision (HPO, MONDO, FLNB genetics)
   - Verify schema alignment
   - Review supporting research documentation

e. Plan of Action:
   - Read the main disorder file and analyze structure/content
   - Check evidence snippets against cached PubMed abstracts
   - Verify ontology term mappings are correct
   - Review research documentation for thoroughness
   - Run validation commands to confirm claims
   - Provide detailed review feedback

f. No anticipated permission issues - this is a review task using standard file reading and validation tools
</analysis>

input.body: ### Dismech Curation Review: Larsen Syndrome Reviewing the Larsen Syndrome curation for quality, evidence integrity, ontology precision, and schema alignment. **Review Checklist:** - [ ] Read and analyze the main disorder file structure - [ ] Validate evidence integrity (PMID references and snippets) - [ ] Check ontology term precision and mappings - [ ] Review schema alignment and compliance - [ ] Examine research documentation quality - [ ] Run validation commands to verify claims - [ ] Provide comprehensive review feedback --- [View job run](https://github.com/monarch-initiative/dismech/actions/runs/22686886065)
